sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The SPACE action caused changes in the bbox [11, 11, 28, 28] which is the white structure area. Let me check what changed by comparing before and after frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after SPACE press
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Check segmentation changes
before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

print("=== BEFORE ===")
for n in before_seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}] children={n['children']}")

print("\n=== AFTER ===")
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}] children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After pressing SPACE:
- The white structure (node 2) moved from cols [11,25] to cols [14,28] - shifted right by 3
- The red blocks (nodes 3, 4) that were below the white structure at rows [26,28] moved to rows [14,16] and [20,22] at cols [11,13] - they moved to the LEFT side of the white structure
- The green structure and its red blocks (nodes 5, 6, 7, 8) stayed the same
So SPACE seems to rotate or rearrange the white structure with its associated red blocks. Let me look at the ASCII to understand the new configuration.

print("=== WHITE STRUCTURE AREA ===")
for r in range(11, 29):
    print(f"{r:2d}: {ascii_lines[r][11:29]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Now I see the pattern. The white structure rotated 90 degrees clockwise:
- Originally it was a 15x15 square with a U-shape opening at the bottom
- After rotation, the opening is now on the right side
- The red blocks that were below the white structure are now on the LEFT side (where the opening was before)
So SPACE rotates the white structure. The red blocks seem to follow the rotation pattern.
